description: Whole garden-map object sets at five complexity levels — small, ordinary, large, pathological, and accessibility — decoded independently by the web and iOS clients from the same wire JSON and checked against the same expected projection, proving the two clients agree on what a real map document means. Covers every one of the 13 garden object categories at least once (see the 'pathological' and 'ordinary' cases for the ones 'ordinary' alone does not reach), and exercises multi-part geometries (MultiPolygon/MultiLineString), nested geometry inside details (a tree canopy), large coordinate magnitudes, long labels, unicode text, boundary-value numerics, and soft-deleted objects.

cases.name: small
cases.description: A brand-new garden with only its lot boundary drawn.

cases.name: ordinary
cases.description: A typical suburban garden: lot, house, fence, gate, path, bed, zone, tree, and an assigned plant.

cases.name: large
cases.description: A large planting bed: 65 objects total.

cases.name: pathological
cases.description: Extreme but individually valid geometries, values, and nested structures.

details.notes: Far corner of a very large rural lot.

objects.label: A greenhouse with an unusually long, fully-written description used to make sure long labels survive decoding on both platforms without truncation or crashing
Old oak with a mapped canopy
